channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: Highly tunable Kondo effect with competing magnetism in the kagome lattice compound ${\mathrm{CsCr}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustration-induced flat band systems offer a unique pathway to realize Kondo physics. Here, the authors use scanning tunneling spectroscopy and thermodynamic probes to reveal highly tunable Kondo effect in the kagome compound CsCr6Sb6. They show that temperature and in-plane magnetic field tune the competition between Kondo effect and underlying magnetism, establishing this compound as a new paradigm for studying correlated quantum states in 3d electron systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155128] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustration-induced flat band systems offer a unique pathway to realize Kondo physics. Here, the authors use scanning tunneling spectroscopy and thermodynamic probes to reveal highly tunable Kondo effect in the kagome compound CsCr6Sb6. They show that temperature and in-plane magnetic field tune the competition between Kondo effect and underlying magnetism, establishing this compound as a new paradigm for studying correlated quantum states in 3d electron systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155128] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Highly tunable Kondo effect with competing magnetism in the kagome lattice compound ${\mathrm{CsCr}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

item.title: Fractional magnetization plateaus and nontrivial magnetic ground state in the kagome antiferromagnet $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

In this study, we report the fractional magnetization plateaus and anomalous Hall effect in a newly synthesized kagome intermetallic magnet, $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$. In this compound, the Ce atoms form a distorted kagome lattice, which allows for strong magnetic frus…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144105] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

In this study, we report the fractional magnetization plateaus and anomalous Hall effect in a newly synthesized kagome intermetallic magnet, Ce3MgBi5. In this compound, the Ce atoms form a distorted kagome lattice, which allows for strong magnetic frustration by Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida interac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144105] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractional magnetization plateaus and nontrivial magnetic ground state in the kagome antiferromagnet $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

item.title: Disorder-enhanced quantum transport in twisted bilayer graphene near the magic angle
item.description:
Author(s): Zhe Hou, Hailong Li, Qing Yan, Yu-Hang Li, and Hua Jiang

In disordered lattices, itinerant electrons typically undergo Anderson localization due to random phase interference, which suppresses their motion. By contrast, in flat-band systems where electrons are intrinsically localized owing to their vanishing group velocity, the role of disorder remains elu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhe Hou, Hailong Li, Qing Yan, Yu-Hang Li, and Hua Jiang

In disordered lattices, itinerant electrons typically undergo Anderson localization due to random phase interference, which suppresses their motion. By contrast, in flat-band systems where electrons are intrinsically localized owing to their vanishing group velocity, the role of disorder remains elu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Disorder-enhanced quantum transport in twisted bilayer graphene near the magic angle
item.dc:creator: Zhe Hou, Hailong Li, Qing Yan, Yu-Hang Li, and Hua Jiang

item.title: Excitonic correlations in the equilibrium and voltage-biased bilayer Hubbard model: Multiorbital two-particle self-consistent approach
item.description:
Author(s): Jiawei Yan, Jonas B. Profe, Yuta Murakami, and Philipp Werner

We develop a nonequilibrium multiorbital extension of the two-particle self-consistent theory and apply it to the bilayer Hubbard model as a minimal platform to investigate correlation effects in the presence of interlayer interactions and tunneling. The method determines vertex corrections in the s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165120] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiawei Yan, Jonas B. Profe, Yuta Murakami, and Philipp Werner

We develop a nonequilibrium multiorbital extension of the two-particle self-consistent theory and apply it to the bilayer Hubbard model as a minimal platform to investigate correlation effects in the presence of interlayer interactions and tunneling. The method determines vertex corrections in the s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165120] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Excitonic correlations in the equilibrium and voltage-biased bilayer Hubbard model: Multiorbital two-particle self-consistent approach

item.title: Mode-selective cloaking and phase-matching cavity resonances in bilayer graphene transport
item.description:
Author(s): Dan-Na Liu, Jun Zheng, and Pierre A. Pantaleón

We study ballistic electron transport through electrostatic barriers in AB-stacked bilayer graphene within a full four-band framework. A mode-resolved analysis reveals how propagating and evanescent channels couple across electrostatic interfaces and how channel selectivity governs transport at norm…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165412] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dan-Na Liu, Jun Zheng, and Pierre A. Pantaleón

We study ballistic electron transport through electrostatic barriers in AB-stacked bilayer graphene within a full four-band framework. A mode-resolved analysis reveals how propagating and evanescent channels couple across electrostatic interfaces and how channel selectivity governs transport at norm…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165412] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Mode-selective cloaking and phase-matching cavity resonances in bilayer graphene transport

item.title: Doublon-holon pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Ryota Ueda, Madhumita Sarkar, Zala Lenarčič, Denis Golež, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Tatsuya Kaneko

We demonstrate the existence of an unconventional pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators on ladder and quasi-two-dimensional geometries, characterized by quasi-long-range doublon-holon correlations that signal Mott exciton condensation. The doublon-holon pairing exhibits correlations of $d$-wav…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161109] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryota Ueda, Madhumita Sarkar, Zala Lenarčič, Denis Golež, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Tatsuya Kaneko

We demonstrate the existence of an unconventional pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators on ladder and quasi-two-dimensional geometries, characterized by quasi-long-range doublon-holon correlations that signal Mott exciton condensation. The doublon-holon pairing exhibits correlations of d-wave-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161109] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Doublon-holon pairing state in photodoped Mott insulators
item.dc:creator: Ryota Ueda, Madhumita Sarkar, Zala Lenarčič, Denis Golež, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Tatsuya Kaneko

item.title: Skyrmionic transport and first-order phase transitions in a twisted bilayer graphene quantum Hall ferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Vineet Pandey, Prasenjit Ghosh, Riju Pal, Sourav Paul, Abhijith M B, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Atindra Nath Pal, and Vidya Kochat

Large-angle twisted bilayer graphene (TBLG) realizes a multicomponent quantum Hall (QH) platform of spin, valley, and layer pseudospins with strong Coulomb interaction-driven symmetry broken phases. Here, we investigate the low energy Landau-level spectrum of layer-decoupled TBLG and identify skyrmi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161110] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vineet Pandey, Prasenjit Ghosh, Riju Pal, Sourav Paul, Abhijith M B, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Atindra Nath Pal, and Vidya Kochat

Large-angle twisted bilayer graphene (TBLG) realizes a multicomponent quantum Hall (QH) platform of spin, valley, and layer pseudospins with strong Coulomb interaction-driven symmetry broken phases. Here, we investigate the low energy Landau-level spectrum of layer-decoupled TBLG and identify skyrmi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161110] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Skyrmionic transport and first-order phase transitions in a twisted bilayer graphene quantum Hall ferromagnet

item.title: Investigating the Fermi-Hubbard model by the tensor-backflow method
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao Liang

Recently, a variational wave function based on the tensor representation of backflow corrections along with a Lanczos step has achieved state-of-the-art energy precision for Fermi-Hubbard-type models. However, solving the Fermi-Hubbard model remains challenging, and the validity of any method must b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155121] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao Liang

Recently, a variational wave function based on the tensor representation of backflow corrections along with a Lanczos step has achieved state-of-the-art energy precision for Fermi-Hubbard-type models. However, solving the Fermi-Hubbard model remains challenging, and the validity of any method must b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155121] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Investigating the Fermi-Hubbard model by the tensor-backflow method

item.title: Dynamical correlations and nonequilibrium sum rules in photodoped Hubbard ladders
item.description:
Author(s): E. Merhej, J. P. Hague, R. M. Konik, and A. J. A. James

Using matrix product state techniques we study the nonequilibrium dynamical response of the half-filled Hubbard ladder when subject to an optical pump. Optical pumping offers a way of producing and manipulating new strongly correlated phenomena by suppressing existing magnetic correlations. The ladd…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165119] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): E. Merhej, J. P. Hague, R. M. Konik, and A. J. A. James

Using matrix product state techniques we study the nonequilibrium dynamical response of the half-filled Hubbard ladder when subject to an optical pump. Optical pumping offers a way of producing and manipulating new strongly correlated phenomena by suppressing existing magnetic correlations. The ladd…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165119] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Exotic transition and reentrance on the 1/9 magnetization plateau in a spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Da-zhi Fang, Shi-Ju Ran, and Gang Su

With the intriguing coexistence of local magnetic orders and nonlocal valence bond patterns, unconventional magnetic plateau phases in geometrically frustrated quantum systems have attracted significant attention. This work investigates the spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet, uncovering an …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140405] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Da-zhi Fang, Shi-Ju Ran, and Gang Su

With the intriguing coexistence of local magnetic orders and nonlocal valence bond patterns, unconventional magnetic plateau phases in geometrically frustrated quantum systems have attracted significant attention. This work investigates the spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet, uncovering an …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140405] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Sliding barrier effects on magnetoresistance and spin-transfer torque in $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{GaT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$-based magnetic tunnel junctions
item.description:
Author(s): Baochun Wu, Jie Yang, Shiqi Liu, and Jing Lu

Electronic properties of van der Waals materials are highly sensitive to their stacking modes. To explore sliding effects in the two-dimensional (2D) spintronics, we construct a magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) using sliding bilayer graphene or $h$-BN as barriers and the recently synthesized room-temp…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134415] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Baochun Wu, Jie Yang, Shiqi Liu, and Jing Lu

Electronic properties of van der Waals materials are highly sensitive to their stacking modes. To explore sliding effects in the two-dimensional (2D) spintronics, we construct a magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) using sliding bilayer graphene or h-BN as barriers and the recently synthesized room-temper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134415] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Sliding barrier effects on magnetoresistance and spin-transfer torque in $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{GaT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$-based magnetic tunnel junctions
item.dc:creator: Baochun Wu, Jie Yang, Shiqi Liu, and Jing Lu

item.title: Resonant dynamics of one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbard model with time-dependent tensor electric fields
item.description:
Author(s): Jiali Zhang and Shaoliang Zhang

Recently, tensor gauge fields and their coupling to fracton phases of matter have attracted more and more research interest, and a series of novel quantum phenomena arising from the coupling has been predicted. Here, we propose a tunable platform using a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144305] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiali Zhang and Shaoliang Zhang

Recently, tensor gauge fields and their coupling to fracton phases of matter have attracted more and more research interest, and a series of novel quantum phenomena arising from the coupling has been predicted. Here, we propose a tunable platform using a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144305] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026

item.title: Mechanism of the Mott-like insulator-to-metal transition of heavy-ion irradiated $\mathrm{Cr}X{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ ($X=\mathrm{Si}$ and Ge)
item.description:
Author(s): Siyue Zhang, Yasuhiro Niwa, Haruto Ejiri, Jun Fujioka, Kazuya Harii, Satoru Okayasu, and Yuki Shiomi

It was recently reported that heavy-ion irradiation to layered ferromagnetic insulators $\mathrm{Cr}X{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ ($X=\mathrm{Si}$ and Ge) produces amorphous ferromagnetic metals with approximately three times higher transition temperatures (${T}_{C}$) than those of single crystals. This high-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155116] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Siyue Zhang, Yasuhiro Niwa, Haruto Ejiri, Jun Fujioka, Kazuya Harii, Satoru Okayasu, and Yuki Shiomi

It was recently reported that heavy-ion irradiation to layered ferromagnetic insulators CrXTe3 (X=Si and Ge) produces amorphous ferromagnetic metals with approximately three times higher transition temperatures (TC) than those of single crystals. This high-TC metallic state is reminiscent of the cor…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155116] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Mechanism of the Mott-like insulator-to-metal transition of heavy-ion irradiated $\mathrm{Cr}X{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ ($X=\mathrm{Si}$ and Ge)
